### Time-Dynamic AI Models in Breast Cancer Care: Enhancing Personalized Predictions of Health-Related Quality of Life Impairments

#### Introduction
Breast cancer patients frequently experience health-related quality of life (HRQoL) impairments that can significantly impact their overall well-being and quality of life. These impairments are challenging to predict on an individual basis, making it difficult for clinicians to provide targeted interventions. Traditional prognostic models, which use fixed intervals, have limitations in capturing the dynamic nature of HRQoL changes over time. This study aims to develop and validate time-dynamic machine learning (ML) models that predict clinically relevant HRQoL impairments in nonmetastatic breast cancer patients.

#### Methodology
The study utilized the European Organization for Research and Treatment of Cancer (EORTC) BALANCE dataset, which included 6316 patients with repeated HRQoL assessments (EORTC QLQ-C30). Over 70,000 patient assessment pairs were analyzed. ML algorithms were trained using earlier HRQoL assessments and clinical data to predict dichotomized impairments in QLQ-C30 domains at various follow-up intervals ranging from 2 weeks to 5 years. The best-performing model was selected based on its area under the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve (AUC) in internal validation and then externally validated in an independent cohort.
